brief_document.title: Common wire types, proto<->core conversions, and the WireError taxonomy
brief_document.purpose: Define the shared primitives every other message family uses: the proto representations of aion-core's identifiers, payload, and status; the lossless conversions between proto and aion-core types; and the WireError taxonomy that turns the engine's and store's typed errors into stable, language-neutral codes.
brief_document.task: Add common.proto declaring proto messages for WorkflowId, RunId, ActivityId, TimerId, Payload, WorkflowStatus, and a thin envelope that wraps a serialised aion-core value with a namespace and request id. Write convert.rs giving infallible-where-possible, lossless conversions between these proto messages and aion-core types. Define WireError as a closed taxonomy with stable codes and write the mapping from aion's EngineError, aion-store's StoreError, and query/signal/namespace failures onto it. Reuse aion-core's Event/WorkflowFilter/WorkflowSummary directly via the envelope — do not clone them as wire structs.

requirements.title: Common proto messages and core conversions
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define proto messages for WorkflowId, RunId, ActivityId, TimerId, Payload, and WorkflowStatus, and SHALL provide lossless conversion between each and its aion-core counterpart.
acceptance: common.proto defines messages mirroring aion-core WorkflowId, RunId, ActivityId, TimerId, Payload, and WorkflowStatus.
convert.rs implements From/TryFrom both directions for each type; a unit test round-trips every type proto->core->proto and core->proto->core and asserts equality.
Payload conversion preserves the content-type tag and bytes exactly (lossless for all JSON value kinds, mirroring aion-core's Payload guarantee).
Conversions that cannot fail use From; conversions that can (malformed proto) use TryFrom returning a WireError.

requirements.title: Envelope reuses aion-core types; no wire clones
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL carry aion-core Event, WorkflowFilter, and WorkflowSummary across the wire via a thin envelope (namespace, request id, serialised aion-core value), NOT via re-declared wire-clone structs.
acceptance: A WireEnvelope (or equivalent) carries a namespace, an optional request id, and a serialised aion-core value (e.g. a Payload-encoded or bytes-encoded core type).
Event, WorkflowFilter, and WorkflowSummary are serialised from aion-core's own serde derives, not from any wire-specific duplicate type.
A grep/inspection confirms aion-proto declares no struct that re-fields Event, WorkflowFilter, WorkflowSummary, or WorkflowStatus (per CO12).
A unit test round-trips an aion-core Event of at least one variant through the envelope and asserts equality.

requirements.title: WireError closed taxonomy
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL define WireError as a closed taxonomy carrying a stable code and a human message, covering not-found, namespace-denied, sequence-conflict, unknown-query, query-timeout, not-running, lagged, and backend categories.
acceptance: WireError has variants (with stable integer or string codes) for NotFound, NamespaceDenied, SequenceConflict, UnknownQuery, QueryTimeout, NotRunning, Lagged, and Backend.
WireError derives Serialize + Deserialize, implements std::error::Error and Display, and is Send + Sync.
Each code is documented as stable so client SDKs may branch on it; the human message is informational only.
WireError is representable as a proto message in common.proto and round-trips losslessly.

requirements.title: Mapping engine and store errors onto WireError
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL map aion's EngineError, aion-store's StoreError, and query/signal/namespace failures onto WireError, never exposing Debug-formatted internals on the wire.
acceptance: A documented mapping turns each EngineError and StoreError variant into a specific WireError code (e.g. StoreError::SequenceConflict -> WireError::SequenceConflict, EngineError unknown-workflow -> NotFound).
Namespace authorisation failures map to NamespaceDenied; query timeout and unknown-query map to QueryTimeout and UnknownQuery; a terminated/unknown workflow maps to NotRunning.
No mapping path forwards a Debug or Display of the underlying error verbatim as the code; the human message is the only free-text field (per D3).
A unit test asserts representative mappings for an EngineError and a StoreError variant.

The mapping lives in aion-proto and references aion-store's StoreError; it references aion's EngineError type only structurally — note that if EngineError is not reachable from a leaf crate, the mapping for engine errors is provided as a documented conversion the server (AW-008/AW-009) applies, and aion-proto owns only the StoreError + namespace + query/signal mappings. State which split is used.

boundaries: SHALL NOT define the workflow, events, or worker service messages — AW-003, AW-004, AW-005.
SHALL NOT depend on aion (the engine crate); if EngineError mapping cannot live in a leaf crate, the engine-error mapping is applied server-side and documented as such (R4 records the split).
SHALL NOT re-declare Event/WorkflowFilter/WorkflowSummary/WorkflowStatus as wire-clone structs (CO12).
SHALL NOT implement any server handler or transport — those are AW-008 onward.
verification: No #[allow], #[expect], or #[ignore] directives in new code.
Inspection confirms no wire-clone of aion-core's Event/Filter/Summary/Status types exists.

checklist.text: common.proto and convert.rs define the proto<->aion-core mapping for WorkflowId, RunId, ActivityId, TimerId, Payload, and WorkflowStatus with lossless round-trip.
The wire Event/WorkflowFilter/WorkflowSummary reuse aion-core's types via a thin envelope (namespace, request id, serialised core payload); no wire clone of those types exists.
WireError is a closed taxonomy with stable codes covering not-found, namespace-denied, sequence-conflict, unknown-query, query-timeout, not-running, lagged, and backend categories.
WireError maps from aion's EngineError, aion-store's StoreError, and query/signal/namespace failures, with a documented mapping and no Debug-formatted internals on the wire.

stories.text: As a client SDK author, I want failures to arrive as stable error codes so that my SDK can branch on 'workflow not found' versus 'namespace denied' without parsing strings.
As a worker SDK author, I want my build to depend only on the wire contract and the domain types, never the engine, so that my worker binary stays small and decoupled.

constraints.text: No #[allow]/#[expect]/#[ignore] lint bypasses per CLAUDE.md; tests needing a running server gate at runtime (a *_TEST_URL env var with a logged skip), never #[ignore].
lib.rs/mod.rs are declarations and re-exports only.
aion-proto is a leaf crate: it depends on aion-core and external crates only — no aion, no aion-server, no store backend. Structural; must hold (per D2).
aion-server depends on aion, aion-proto, and a store backend among workspace crates; it contains no workflow execution logic — it delegates every durable decision to the Engine (per D5).
Wire data types (proto messages, streamed events, summaries) derive Serialize + Deserialize; config types that carry secrets are Deserialize-only (D4, project credential policy).
Library errors use thiserror (WireError in proto, ServerError in server); anyhow appears only in aion-server's top-level main. No unwrap/expect in library code; lock/stream poison handled explicitly.
No cross-namespace operation reaches the engine: namespace authorisation and scoping happen at the adapter boundary before any Engine call (per D8).
A slow or stalled WebSocket consumer is dropped with a typed lag error; per-connection buffers are bounded; the engine broadcast is never back-pressured by a subscriber (per D6).
No hardcoded operational defaults (ports, heartbeat window, buffer bound, timeouts): all from config; engine-owned defaults deferred to the engine (per CLAUDE.md 'no assumed defaults').
The wire Event/WorkflowFilter/WorkflowSummary/WorkflowStatus and ID types are aion-core's types, not re-declared wire clones (per D2).

resolved_context.intention: Build the network skin of Aion. The aion engine crate is transport-agnostic by design — no HTTP, gRPC, or WebSocket — so a CLI embeds it with nothing but a file-backed store. Teams running many services want a managed deployment: one binary clients and workers point at, that streams live workflow events to a dashboard, isolates tenants, and dispatches activity tasks to out-of-process workers in any language. That is aion-server, and the wire contract it speaks is aion-proto. aion-proto is a pure leaf-level contract — gRPC service definitions plus serde wire types — that the server, every client SDK (AL), and every worker SDK (AR) agree on, defined once so they never drift. aion-server is the deployable: it wraps a live Engine, translates wire requests into Engine calls, tails the engine's event broadcast to feed WebSocket subscribers, runs the server side of the remote-worker protocol, partitions everything by namespace, and serves the dashboard's API and static assets. It adds no execution logic — every durable decision still happens in aion.
